item.title: Defence spending won’t solve Europe’s growth problem
item.description: Plus, what does a healthy labour market look like when there is no job growth?

item.title: The CEO chatbot era is coming
item.description: Many executives will be tempted by Meta’s plans for AI version of Mark Zuckerberg with interest

item.title: The mix of chaotic politics and a resilient economy can’t last
item.description: The world’s economy has managed to withstand a great deal of chaos, but new IMF forecasts show that things could get worse

item.title: Oil shortages are coming, and with them some difficult questions
item.description: It may not be the early sufferers who experience the most pain if the Strait of Hormuz stays closed for a prolonged period

item.title: Middle powers make like an armadillo in a challenging world
item.description: Would-be architects of a future global order should take heed

item.title: Betting on risk changes the world
item.description: Prediction markets are the latest example of a disruptive new industry smacking into problems incumbents have long understood

item.title: Musk is officially free to roam in Texas
item.description: Delaware ruling draws a line under Tesla’s corporate domicile in the Lone Star State

item.title: America’s tightening cost of living squeeze
item.description: The Iran war has intensified US households’ affordability struggles

item.title: Ad giant Publicis is less ‘Mad Men’, more ‘The IT Crowd’
item.description: Investors are not buying the French group’s AI-fuelled growth story

item.title: The US is at risk of an oil shock too
item.description: In a global market for crude, Asian buyers are vacuuming up supplies, raising prices for Europe and America

item.title: As Trump scares off US scientists, China is racing ahead
item.description: From mapping the seabed to AI innovation, Beijing is becoming the world’s scientific superpower

item.title: I’m getting divorced. Should I settle out of court?
item.description: I would like to know the benefits and drawbacks of a financial dispute resolution

item.title: The ever-shrinking JD Vance
item.description: After a series of setbacks, the vice-president is no longer Trump’s obvious successor

item.title: The Vix indicator of the time to buy
item.description: A rise in the equity market’s fear gauge is usually a signal of a rally to come

item.title: Small caps revisited
item.description: Index choice matters, and so do valuations

item.title: Mythos and the cyber security risk facing all states
item.description: AI is like the atomic bomb — once you invent the means to build one, you live in a different world

item.title: Why ‘glue work’ can finally shine in the age of AI
item.description: Automating technical skills will transform the value of those who can build relationships and hold projects together

item.title: Ask a stylist: flats have finally got comfy and cool. How do I choose?
item.description: Mary Janes, smoking slippers and fisherman sandals are smarter alternatives to sneakers

item.title: The end of the Viktor Orbán era
item.description: Populist nationalism can be beaten at the ballot box, even if it can endure a long time

item.title: Goldman Sachs retains knack for spinning anxiety into gold
item.description: AI-inspired stock routs, the war in Iran and private credit were no hindrance to Wall Street bank

item.title: Hungary’s election could support Europe’s revival trade
item.description: In the long run, the result may be felt far beyond Budapest

item.title: Retirement could be the most social time of your life
item.description: New research challenges the narrative that ageing societies should be thought of in terms of decline

item.title: The price of marriage in Switzerland
item.description: Social conservatism in this otherwise egalitarian country extends to tax penalties for couples who both work

item.title: Battery giant CATL is no longer just a play on electric vehicles
item.description: The Chinese group’s expansion into non-automotive areas will benefit from policy-driven demand to offset consumer cycles

item.title: Why time is on Iran’s side
item.description: A global energy crisis is only just beginning. Political turmoil will follow
